channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Argentina's 2018-19 hantavirus outbreak is guiding the response to this one
item.description: The outbreak in Argentina in 2018-19 of hantavirus is one that scientists studied carefully, so many researchers are turning to it for information about the virus.
item.content:encoded: The outbreak in Argentina in 2018-19 of hantavirus is one that scientists studied carefully, so many researchers are turning to it for information about the virus.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Omaha's U.S. House race will test candidates' ability to reach beyond party lines
item.description: Omaha, Neb., is home to a toss-up Congressional race this year. Voters have shown a willingness to vote for moderate candidates from both parties, but has the definition of a moderate now changed?
item.content:encoded: Omaha, Neb., is home to a toss-up Congressional race this year. Voters have shown a willingness to vote for moderate candidates from both parties, but has the definition of a moderate now changed?
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters

item.title: Sam Altman's testimony begins in Musk trial
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman takes the stand to defend his company against a lawsuit brought by Elon Musk.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman takes the stand to defend his company against a lawsuit brought by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Myrow

item.title: Farmers want firm trade deal from Trump's China visit
item.description: President Trump's state visit to China will be closely watched in the American heartland, where his trade war has resulted in grain farmers losing considerable business from international buyers.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's state visit to China will be closely watched in the American heartland, where his trade war has resulted in grain farmers losing considerable business from international buyers.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: Inflation is up. Americans explain how it's affecting them
item.description: Consumer prices surged 3.8% in April from a year ago, according to the Labor Department. We hear from people who are feeling the effects of inflation and how they're dealing.
item.content:encoded: Consumer prices surged 3.8% in April from a year ago, according to the Labor Department. We hear from people who are feeling the effects of inflation and how they're dealing.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: Cure for Paranoia shares what it took to win's this year's Tiny Desk Contest
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Cameron McCloud of the band Cure for Paranoia, which won this year's Tiny Desk Contest.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Cameron McCloud of the band Cure for Paranoia, which won this year's Tiny Desk Contest.
item.dc:creator: Tinbete Ermyas

item.title: How Americans see China now
item.description: As President Trump heads to China this week, a new NPR-Chicago Council-Ipsos poll finds most Americans think U.S. tariffs have hurt both economies, and that the Iran war is bad for America.
item.content:encoded: As President Trump heads to China this week, a new NPR-Chicago Council-Ipsos poll finds most Americans think U.S. tariffs have hurt both economies, and that the Iran war is bad for America.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: The clipping economy: How short-form video 'clippers' are overrunningthe internet
item.description: Turning long-form podcasts and interviews into short-form social media clips has become a lucrative career for some. But others say it is a race to the bottom.
item.content:encoded: Turning long-form podcasts and interviews into short-form social media clips has become a lucrative career for some. But others say it is a race to the bottom.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: New treatments offer hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
item.description: Although pancreatic cancer remains very lethal, a few new kinds of therapies are coming
item.content:encoded: Although pancreatic cancer remains very lethal, a few new kinds of therapies are coming
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: In '2084,' authors fictionalize global threats as a real world warning
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks to Admiral James Stavridis and Elliot Ackerman about their new book, 2084, which examines a future where climate change has ravaged the planet.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks to Admiral James Stavridis and Elliot Ackerman about their new book, 2084, which examines a future where climate change has ravaged the planet.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly
